abstract: This essay in honor of ASQ's 70th volume surveys how technology-driven changes in scholarly publishing have introduced algorithmic management to organizational research. The internet greatly reduced the cost of publishing journals and prompted an orders-of-magnitude increase in the number of journals and articles while also foregrounding quantitative metrics for scholarship. Given the academic incentive system of publish or perish, the new online ecosystem has encouraged problematic practices by scholars and publishers that threaten the standards and values of organizational theory. The advent of generative artificial intelligence within this milieu is almost certain to worsen the publishing trends we have already experienced. Drawing on prior literature about the centrality of deep intellectual engagement through reading, writing, and interactions with colleagues, we propose a set of reforms to preserve the sacredness of craft and community at the core of our scholarly work.

ob.ai_reason: 直接讨论生成式AI和算法管理对学术出版的影响，核心AI议题
ob.tldr: 研究技术驱动出版变革将算法管理引入学术组织，生成式AI加剧期刊数量激增与量化指标主导，导致学者与出版商不当行为危及组织理论标准。基于深度阅读、写作、同行互动的学术工艺精神，呼吁改革以维护学术社区核心价值。

title: Resisting the Algorithmic Management of Science: Craft and Community After Generative AI
title_zh: 抵制科学的算法管理：生成式人工智能之后的工艺与社区
